entries.definition:
dh–l–l mercy, kindness; to become humbled, to become humiliated; to be tamed, to become docile; to be within easy reach. Of this root, 12 forms occur 24 times in the Qur’an: نَدْهِل nadhill once; ذَلَّلَ dhallala once; ذُلِّلَ dhullila once; تُذِلُّ tudhillu once; تَذْلِيل tadhlīl once; ذُلّ dhull three times; ذِلَّة dhillatun seven times; أَذِلَّة ʾadhillatun four times; أَذَلُّ ʾadhall once; أَذَلُّونَ ʾadhallūn once; ذَلُول dhalūl twice and ذُلُل dhulul once.
333
entries.definition_english:
dh–l–l mercy, kindness; to become humbled, to become humiliated; to be tamed, to become docile; to be within easy reach. Of this root, 12 forms occur 24 times in the Qur’an: نَدْهِل nadhill once; ذَلَّلَ dhallala once; ذُلِّلَ dhullila once; تُذِلُّ tudhillu once; تَذْلِيل tadhlīl once; ذُلّ dhull three times; ذِلَّة dhillatun seven times; أَذِلَّة ʾadhillatun four times; أَذَلُّ ʾadhall once; أَذَلُّونَ ʾadhallūn once; ذَلُول dhalūl twice and ذُلُل dhulul once.
333

entries.definition:
نَدْهِل nadhill [imperf. of ذَلَّ dhalla, intrans.] to be humiliated, to be abject, to become humbled (20:134) رَبَّنَا لَوْلَا أَرْسَلْتَ إِلَيْنَا رَسُولًا فَنَتَّبِعَ ءَايَاتِكَ مِن قَبْلِ أَن نَّذِلَّ وَنَخْزَىٰ Lord, if only You had sent us a messenger we could have followed Your revelations before we suffered humiliation and disgrace!
ذَلَّلَ dhallala [v. II, trans.] to tame, to cause to be docile, to cause to be manageable (36:72) وَذَلَّلْنَاهَا لَهُمْ فَمِنْهَا رَكُوبُهُمْ وَمِنْهَا يَأْكُلُونَ and We have made [livestock] manageable for them, so that some [can be used] for riding and some they [can] eat.

ذُلِّلَ dhullila [pass. of v. II] to be positioned within easy reach, to be made accessible (76:14) وَذُلِّلَتْ قُطُوفُهَا تَذْلِيلًا and its fruit-clusters are made to hang low within easy reach.

تُذِلُّ tudhillu [imperf. of v. IV أَذَلَّ ʾadhalla, trans.] to humiliate, to humble, to abase (3:26) وَتُعِزُّ مَن تَشَاءُ وَتُذِلُّ مَن تَشَاءُ You elevate whomsoever You will and humble whomsoever You will.

تَذْلِيل tadhlīl [v. n.] the act of facilitating, placing within easy reach (76:14) وَذُلِّلَتْ قُطُوفُهَا تَذْلِيلًا and its fruit-clusters are made to hang low within easy reach.

ذُلّ dhull [n./v. n.] 1 humility, humbleness (17:24) وَاخْفِضْ لَهُمَا جَنَاحَ الذُّلِّ مِنَ الرَّحْمَةِ and humble yourself, be tender to them [lit. lower for them, the wing of humbleness], out of mercy 2 abasement, humiliation (42:45) وَتَرَاهُمْ يُعْرَضُونَ عَلَيْهَا خَاشِعِينَ مِنَ الذُّلِّ you will see them exposed to it, [made] humble by humiliation.

ذِلَّة dhillatun [n.] humiliation, disgrace, shame, abasement (10:26) وَلَا يَرْهَقُ وُجُوهَهُمْ قَتَرٌ وَلَا ذِلَّةٌ and neither darkness nor humiliation shall cover their faces.

أَذِلَّة ʾadhillatun [pl. of quasi-pass. part. ذَلِيل dhalīl] 1 weak (3:123) وَلَقَدْ نَصَرَكُمُ اللَّهُ بِبَدْرٍ وَأَنتُمْ أَذِلَّةٌ and God indeed helped you at Badr when you were weak 2 humble (5:54) فَسَوْفَ يَأْتِي اللَّهُ بِقَوْمٍ يُحِبُّهُمْ وَيُحِبُّونَهُ أَذِلَّةٍ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ God will assuredly bring forth a people He loves and who love Him, humble towards the believers 3 abased, humiliated (27:37) فَلَنَأْتِيَنَّهُم بِجُنُودٍ لَّا قِبَلَ لَهُم بِهَا وَلَنُخْرِجَنَّهُم مِّنْهَا أَذِلَّةً وَهُمْ صَاغِرُونَ we will certainly come upon them with forces they have no power to face and we will drive them out of it, abased and

334
